name: Dialog
description: A dialog is a modal window for a focused task or decision that interrupts the operator on purpose — confirming a deletion, capturing a few fields, or surfacing something that needs a choice before continuing. Built from @mui/material/Dialog composed with DialogTitle/DialogContent/DialogActions, not a single all-in-one props API.
keyRule: Use a dialog only when the task genuinely needs to interrupt. For routine detail, prefer a Drawer (temporary variant) or a detail page. For transient confirmation, prefer a Snackbar.

variants.name: Alert / confirmation
variants.whenToUse: Confirm a consequential action (delete, discard). Use role="alertdialog".

variants.name: Form dialog
variants.whenToUse: Capture a few fields without leaving the page.

variants.name: Full-screen (rare on desktop)
variants.whenToUse: A larger task that benefits from focus; usually a page is better. Set fullScreen.

anatomy.name: Backdrop
anatomy.description: the dimmed scrim behind the dialog, rendered by the underlying Modal (MUI's real slot: `backdrop`).

anatomy.name: Paper
anatomy.description: the dialog surface itself — MUI's real `paper` slot, a Paper component (4px radius, elevated).

anatomy.name: DialogTitle
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component you place as the first child — a short, specific question or task name.

anatomy.name: DialogContent
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component wrapping supporting text and/or fields.

anatomy.name: DialogActions
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component wrapping confirm + dismiss buttons, right-aligned; confirm last.

behaviors.name: Modality
behaviors.description: The dialog traps focus and blocks the page (via the underlying Modal); Escape and a backdrop click call onClose for non-destructive dialogs — guard destructive/unsaved flows by checking the `reason` argument and ignoring backdropClick/escapeKeyDown.

behaviors.name: Action order
behaviors.description: Dismiss on the left, confirm on the right inside DialogActions; destructive confirms use the error colour and clear labels ("Remove", not "OK") — this is layout/labelling convention you apply, not a Dialog prop.

behaviors.name: Keep it short
behaviors.description: One decision or a handful of fields; longer tasks belong on a page.

states.treatment: Paper surface over backdrop; focus trapped inside.
Button state layers on DialogActions' children; visible focus.
Fade transition out; returns focus to the trigger (an app-level concern — Dialog itself doesn't remember the trigger).

specs.value: 4px (semantic.radius.default, via theme.shape.borderRadius on the Paper slot)
MUI's real Paper default (elevation 24) — not overridden in theme.ts
maxWidth prop, default 'sm' (~600px breakpoint cap); set fullWidth to stretch to that cap, or maxWidth=false to disable capping
error colour (#F44336 / semantic.color.status.error.main) — applied to the confirm Button's color prop, not built into Dialog

props.name: open
props.description: Narrowed from MUI's real `boolean` by the karoo-ui wrapper — app convention: a Dialog is only ever rendered while open; unmount it to close instead of passing open={false}.

props.name: children
props.description: Compose DialogTitle, DialogContent, and DialogActions as children — there is no separate title/content/confirmLabel prop API.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called on Escape or a backdrop click. Check `reason` to ignore backdrop/Escape dismissal for a destructive, unsaved action rather than closing silently.

props.name: maxWidth
props.description: Caps the dialog width at a breakpoint. Default 'sm'.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Stretches the dialog to the maxWidth cap. Default false.

props.name: fullScreen
props.description: Renders the dialog full-screen — the rare, desktop full-screen variant. Default false.

props.name: scroll
props.description: Where long content scrolls. Default 'paper' (DialogContent scrolls; the Paper stays fixed).

props.name: role
props.description: Use 'alertdialog' for the alert/confirmation variant. Default 'dialog'.

props.name: aria-labelledby
props.description: Should point at DialogTitle's id so the dialog's accessible name is the title.

props.name: aria-describedby
props.description: Should point at DialogContentText's id for the dialog's accessible description.

props.name: transitionDuration
props.description: Overrides the Fade transition's timing; the karoo-ui wrapper's default transition is always `in` regardless of this.

props.name: sx
props.description: Standard MUI system prop for one-off style overrides.

doThis: Use a specific title in DialogTitle that states the decision.
Label DialogActions buttons with verbs (Remove/Save), not OK.
Set aria-labelledby/aria-describedby so assistive tech announces title and content.
dontDoThis: Don't use a dialog for routine detail — use a Drawer (temporary) or a page.
Don't let backdropClick/escapeKeyDown dismiss a destructive, unsaved action silently — check the onClose `reason`.
accessibility: MUI sets role=dialog (or alertdialog) with aria-modal=true automatically via the underlying Modal.
Set aria-labelledby to DialogTitle's id and aria-describedby to DialogContentText's id.
Focus is trapped while open and restored to the trigger on close by the underlying Modal/FocusTrap.
Escape closes non-destructive dialogs by default; guard destructive ones via the onClose reason.
Action buttons in DialogActions need clear, discernible names.

related.name: Drawer
related.why: Complex forms or contextual detail that need page context stay in a temporary-variant Drawer

related.name: Menu
related.why: Lighter-weight choice list that doesn't need to block the page
